data.body:
**Completion verification ‚Äî PASS**

All acceptance criteria are met. Evidence grounded in the repository:

**AC1 ‚Äî P1‚ÄìP7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool**
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` implements all eight checks: `p1_readiness_shape` (lines 141‚Äì169), `p2_resolution` (211‚Äì253), `p3_package_existence` (263‚Äì296), `p4_oversize` (305‚Äì337), `p5_task_dag` (341‚Äì398), `p6_ac_quality` (487‚Äì530), `p7_destructive` (547‚Äì576), `p8_reviewability` (580‚Äì623).
- `run_det_floor()` (line 639) wraps every check in `try/except` ‚Üí `abstain` record on error, never aborts the floor ‚Äî ironclad fail-open.
- `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` covers P1 block/pass, P5 cycle-block/leaf/interference-advisory, P7 destructive with/without safeguard, P8 block/pass, fail-open monkeypatch, and P2/P3 never-block + abstain-without-repo.

**AC2 ‚Äî Coverage recorded on the attestation**
- Orchestrator calls `det_floor.det_coverage(det_results)` and stores it in `coverage["det"]` (orchestrator.py line 259), which flows into the returned `plan_review_verdict` dict. The `det_coverage()` function (det_floor.py line 678) emits per-check ran/abstain/status records.

**AC3 ‚Äî DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1**
- `pass3_decide` (passes.py line 390) defaults `blocking_enabled=False` making every LLM-tier criterion advisory by default; blocking requires explicit opt-in per criterion (`default_posture: "advisory"` in criteria_v8.json, e.g. line 39).
- Only DET checks P1, P5-cycle, and P8 carry `blocking=True` findings.

**AC4 (added) ‚Äî P8 reviewability DET check**
- `p8_reviewability` (det_floor.py line 580) computes a token estimate and fails with "ticket too big to review in full; reduce/decompose it" when content (or largest parent+child pairing for containers) exceeds the largest configured context window, even at one-criterion-per-call.
- Tests `test_p8_blocks_when_too_big` and `test_p8_passes_for_normal_plan` confirm both branches.

**AC5 (added) ‚Äî P1‚ÄìP8 run as deterministic exec=DET tier dispatched by orchestrator (no LLM)**
- `orchestrator.py` line 255: `# ‚îÄ‚îÄ DET tier (exec=DET) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ` labels the first-class execution path.
- Lines 256‚Äì259: `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` is called before any LLM pass and with no LLM involvement.
- P8-too-big short-circuits the entire LLM tier (line 263‚Äì267).

**Verdict: PASS** ‚Äî every stated acceptance criterion is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.
